source: RESEARCH.md (Hisn al-Muslim, Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Sunan; verified 2026-05-26)

morning.subtitle: Recited after Fajr prayer until sunrise (window extends to noon if missed).

entries.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa-l-ḥayyu-l-qayyūm, lā taʾkhudhuhu sinatun walā nawm, lahu mā fis-samāwāti wa mā fil-arḍ... wa huwa-l-ʿaliyyu-l-ʿaẓīm.
entries.translation: Allah! There is no deity but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth...
entries.source: Quran 2:255. Hadith of virtue: An-Nasa'i in Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah (no. 100), Ibn As-Sunni (no. 121).

entries.arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۚ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ ۚ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۚ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ۚ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ۚ إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ۚ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
entries.transliteration: Qul huwa-llāhu aḥad... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-l-falaq... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-n-nās...
entries.translation: Say: He is Allah, the One... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind...
entries.source: Quran 112, 113, 114. Hadith of virtue: Abu Dawud 4/322, At-Tirmidhi 5/567.

entries.arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ وَخَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَهُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوءِ الْكِبَرِ، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي الْقَبْرِ
entries.transliteration: Aṣbaḥnā wa aṣbaḥa-l-mulku lillāh, walḥamdu lillāh, lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah...
entries.translation: We have entered the morning and dominion belongs to Allah, praise be to Allah. There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner...
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2088

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma bika aṣbaḥnā wa bika amsaynā wa bika naḥyā wa bika namūtu wa ilayka-n-nushūr.
entries.translation: O Allah, by You we enter the morning, by You we enter the evening, by You we live, by You we die, and to You is the resurrection.
entries.source: At-Tirmidhi 3/142

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ، أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ، وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي، فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma anta rabbī lā ilāha illā ant, khalaqtanī wa anā ʿabduk, wa anā ʿalā ʿahdika wa waʿdika mastaṭaʿt, aʿūdhu bika min sharri mā ṣanaʿt, abūʾu laka biniʿmatika ʿalayy, wa abūʾu bidhanbī, faghfir lī fa-innahu lā yaghfiru-dh-dhunūba illā ant.
entries.translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is no deity but You. You created me and I am Your servant. I keep Your covenant and pledge as best I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge Your favor upon me and I confess my sin. Forgive me, for none forgives sins except You.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 7/150 (no. 6306). Known as Sayyid al-Istighfar — the master of istighfar. Whoever recites it with conviction and dies that day enters Paradise.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَصْبَحْتُ أُشْهِدُكَ وَأُشْهِدُ حَمَلَةَ عَرْشِكَ، وَمَلَائِكَتَكَ، وَجَمِيعَ خَلْقِكَ، أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّداً عَبْدُكَ وَرَسُولُكَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī aṣbaḥtu ushhiduka wa ushhidu ḥamalata ʿarshik wa malāʾikataka wa jamīʿa khalqik, annaka anta-llāhu lā ilāha illā anta waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, wa anna Muḥammadan ʿabduka wa rasūluk.
entries.translation: O Allah, I have entered the morning and call upon You and upon the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels, and all Your creation to bear witness that You are Allah, there is no deity but You alone, You have no partner, and that Muhammad is Your servant and Your Messenger.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/317, an-Nasa'i in Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, al-Bukhari in al-Adab al-Mufrad.

entries.titleEn: Allahumma ma asbaha bi min ni'mah
entries.titleAr: اللهم ما أصبح بي من نعمة
entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَا أَصْبَحَ بِي مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma mā aṣbaḥa bī min niʿmatin aw bi-aḥadin min khalqika fa-minka waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, falaka-l-ḥamdu wa laka-sh-shukr.
entries.translation: O Allah, whatever blessing has come to me or to any of Your creation this morning is from You alone, with no partner; so all praise is to You and all thanks.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/318, an-Nasa'i, Ibn Hibban no. 2361.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَدَنِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي سَمْعِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَصَرِي، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ، وَالْفَقْرِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī badanī, Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī samʿī, Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī baṣarī, lā ilāha illā ant. Allāhumma innī aʿūdhu bika min al-kufri wal-faqr, wa aʿūdhu bika min ʿadhāb-il-qabr, lā ilāha illā ant.
entries.translation: O Allah, grant my body health. O Allah, grant my hearing health. O Allah, grant my sight health. There is no deity but You. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave. There is no deity but You.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/324, Ahmad 5/42, an-Nasa'i no. 22, Ibn As-Sunni no. 69, al-Bukhari in al-Adab al-Mufrad.

entries.titleAr: حسبي الله لا إله إلا هو
entries.arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
entries.transliteration: Ḥasbiya-llāhu lā ilāha illā huwa, ʿalayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbu-l-ʿarshi-l-ʿaẓīm.
entries.translation: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no deity but Him. Upon Him I rely; He is Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/321, Ibn As-Sunni no. 71 (marfu').

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي دِينِي وَدُنْيَايَ وَأَهْلِي وَمَالِي، اللَّهُمَّ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَاتِي وَآمِنْ رَوْعَاتِي، اللَّهُمَّ احْفَظْنِي مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيَّ وَمِنْ خَلْفِي وَعَنْ يَمِينِي وَعَنْ شِمَالِي وَمِنْ فَوْقِي، وَأَعُوذُ بِعَظَمَتِكَ أَنْ أُغْتَالَ مِنْ تَحْتِي
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī asʾaluka-l-ʿafwa wal-ʿāfiyata fid-dunyā wal-ākhirah...
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the hereafter. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion, my worldly life, my family, and my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, protect me from in front and behind, from my right and my left, and from above. I seek refuge in Your Majesty from being killed from beneath me.
entries.source: Sahih Ibn Majah 2/332, Abu Dawud (Ibn Majah's wording).

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءاً أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ʿālima-l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fāṭira-s-samāwāti wal-arḍ, rabba kulli shayʾin wa malīkah...
entries.translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord and Master of all things, I bear witness that there is no deity but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul and from the evil of Satan and his polytheism, and from committing wrong against myself or bringing it upon any Muslim.
entries.source: Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/142, Abu Dawud.

entries.titleEn: Bismillah alladhi la yadurru ma' ismihi shay'
entries.titleAr: بسم الله الذي لا يضر مع اسمه شيء
entries.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Bismi-llāhi-lladhī lā yaḍurru maʿa ismihi shayʾun fil-arḍi walā fis-samāʾi wa huwa-s-samīʿu-l-ʿalīm.
entries.translation: In the name of Allah, with Whose name nothing in the earth or in the heavens can cause harm, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/323, At-Tirmidhi 5/465, Ibn Majah 2/332, Ahmad.

entries.arabic: رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَبِيًّا
entries.transliteration: Raḍītu billāhi rabbā, wa bil-islāmi dīnā, wa bi-Muḥammadin nabiyyā.
entries.translation: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace be upon him) as my Prophet.
entries.source: Ahmad 4/337, an-Nasa'i, Ibn As-Sunni no. 68, At-Tirmidhi 5/465.

entries.arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ، أَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِي كُلَّهُ، وَلَا تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ
entries.transliteration: Yā Ḥayyu yā Qayyūm, bi-raḥmatika astaghīth, aṣliḥ lī shaʾnī kullah, walā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata ʿayn.
entries.translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining, by Your mercy I seek help. Rectify all my affairs and do not entrust me to my soul, not even for the blink of an eye.
entries.source: Al-Hakim 1/545, with al-Albani's verification in Sahihut-Targhib 1/273.

entries.titleEn: Asbahna wa asbaha al-mulk lillah, allahumma inni as'aluka khayra hadha-l-yawm
entries.titleAr: أصبحنا وأصبح الملك لله رب العالمين
entries.arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذَا الْيَوْمِ، فَتْحَهُ، وَنَصْرَهُ، وَنُورَهُ، وَبَرَكَتَهُ، وَهُدَاهُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيهِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ
entries.transliteration: Aṣbaḥnā wa aṣbaḥa-l-mulku lillāhi rabbi-l-ʿālamīn, Allāhumma innī asʾaluka khayra hādha-l-yawm: fatḥah, wa naṣrah, wa nūrah, wa barakatah, wa hudāh, wa aʿūdhu bika min sharri mā fīhi wa sharri mā baʿdah.
entries.translation: We have entered the morning and dominion belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of this day: its victory, help, light, blessings, and guidance; and I seek refuge in You from the evil in it and the evil that comes after it.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/322. Also in Ibn al-Qayyim, Zad al-Ma'ad 2/273.

entries.arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا عَلَى فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ، وَعَلَى كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلَاصِ، وَعَلَى دِينِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، وَعَلَى مِلَّةِ أَبِينَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَنِيفًا مُسْلِمًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
entries.transliteration: Aṣbaḥnā ʿalā fiṭrati-l-islām, wa ʿalā kalimati-l-ikhlāṣ, wa ʿalā dīni nabiyyinā Muḥammadin ṣalla-llāhu ʿalayhi wa sallam, wa ʿalā millati abīnā Ibrāhīm, ḥanīfan musliman, wa mā kāna mina-l-mushrikīn.
entries.translation: We have entered the morning upon the natural disposition of Islam, upon the word of pure devotion, upon the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), and upon the way of our father Ibrahim, who was upright and Muslim, and was not of the polytheists.
entries.source: Ahmad 3/406-7, an-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi 4/209.

entries.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
entries.transliteration: Subḥāna-llāhi wa biḥamdih.
entries.translation: Glory be to Allah and praise is to Him.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 4/2071, Sahih Muslim 4/2071. Whoever says it 100 times morning and evening: no one will come on the Day of Resurrection with anything better, except one who said the same or more.

entries.titleEn: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah
entries.titleAr: لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له
entries.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
entries.transliteration: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr.
entries.translation: There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs all dominion and all praise, and He has power over all things.
entries.countDescription: 10× minimum morning, 100× preferred. Reciting 100× = reward of freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds recorded, 100 sins erased, protection from Satan that day.
entries.source: An-Nasa'i no. 24, Ahmad, Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah; al-Bukhari 4/95 and Muslim 4/2071 for the 100× version.

entries.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، عَدَدَ خَلْقِهِ، وَرِضَا نَفْسِهِ، وَزِنَةَ عَرْشِهِ، وَمِدَادَ كَلِمَاتِهِ
entries.transliteration: Subḥāna-llāhi wa biḥamdih, ʿadada khalqih, wa riḍā nafsih, wa zinata ʿarshih, wa midāda kalimātih.
entries.translation: Glory be to Allah and praise is to Him, by the number of His creation, by His pleasure, by the weight of His Throne, and by the extent of His words.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2090

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا، وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī asʾaluka ʿilman nāfiʿan, wa rizqan ṭayyiban, wa ʿamalan mutaqabbalan.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, wholesome provision, and accepted deeds.
entries.source: Ibn Majah no. 925, Ibn As-Sunni no. 54.

entries.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
entries.transliteration: Astaghfiru-llāha wa atūbu ilayh.
entries.translation: I seek Allah's forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance.
entries.countDescription: 100× throughout the day (commonly grouped with morning adhkar)
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari (via Fath al-Bari 11/101), Sahih Muslim 4/2075. The Prophet did this >70× per day per Bukhari.

entries.titleEn: Allahumma salli wa sallim 'ala nabiyyina Muhammad
entries.titleAr: اللهم صل وسلم على نبينا محمد
entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ṣalli wa sallim ʿalā nabiyyinā Muḥammad.
entries.translation: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.
entries.source: At-Tabarani (reliable chain, per al-Haythami in Majma' az-Zawa'id and al-Albani in Sahih at-Targhib). Whoever does this gains the Prophet's intercession.

evening.subtitle: Recited after Asr until Maghrib (window extends to nightfall). The set mirrors the morning adhkar with 'amsayna' (we entered the evening) substituted for 'asbahna' (we entered the morning), plus one evening-specific dhikr.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa-l-ḥayyu-l-qayyūm, lā taʾkhudhuhu sinatun walā nawm, lahu mā fis-samāwāti wa mā fil-arḍ... wa huwa-l-ʿaliyyu-l-ʿaẓīm.
entries.translation: Allah! There is no deity but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth...
entries.source: Quran 2:255. Hadith of virtue: An-Nasa'i in Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah (no. 100), Ibn As-Sunni (no. 121).

entries.arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۚ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ ۚ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۚ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ۚ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ۚ إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ۚ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
entries.transliteration: Qul huwa-llāhu aḥad... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-l-falaq... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-n-nās...
entries.translation: Say: He is Allah, the One... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind...
entries.source: Quran 112, 113, 114. Hadith of virtue: Abu Dawud 4/322, At-Tirmidhi 5/567.

entries.arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِي هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ وَخَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِي هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهَا، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوءِ الْكِبَرِ، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي الْقَبْرِ
entries.transliteration: Amsaynā wa amsa-l-mulku lillāh, walḥamdu lillāh, lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah...
entries.translation: We have entered the evening and dominion belongs to Allah, praise be to Allah. There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner...
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2088

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma bika amsaynā wa bika aṣbaḥnā wa bika naḥyā wa bika namūtu wa ilayka-l-maṣīr.
entries.translation: O Allah, by You we enter the evening, by You we enter the morning, by You we live, by You we die, and to You is the final return.
entries.source: At-Tirmidhi 3/142

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ، أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ، وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي، فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma anta rabbī lā ilāha illā ant, khalaqtanī wa anā ʿabduk, wa anā ʿalā ʿahdika wa waʿdika mastaṭaʿt, aʿūdhu bika min sharri mā ṣanaʿt, abūʾu laka biniʿmatika ʿalayy, wa abūʾu bidhanbī, faghfir lī fa-innahu lā yaghfiru-dh-dhunūba illā ant.
entries.translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is no deity but You. You created me and I am Your servant. I keep Your covenant and pledge as best I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge Your favor upon me and I confess my sin. Forgive me, for none forgives sins except You.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 7/150 (no. 6306). Known as Sayyid al-Istighfar — the master of istighfar.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَمْسَيْتُ أُشْهِدُكَ وَأُشْهِدُ حَمَلَةَ عَرْشِكَ، وَمَلَائِكَتَكَ، وَجَمِيعَ خَلْقِكَ، أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّداً عَبْدُكَ وَرَسُولُكَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī amsaytu ushhiduka wa ushhidu ḥamalata ʿarshik wa malāʾikataka wa jamīʿa khalqik, annaka anta-llāhu lā ilāha illā anta waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, wa anna Muḥammadan ʿabduka wa rasūluk.
entries.translation: O Allah, I have entered the evening and call upon You and upon the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels, and all Your creation to bear witness that You are Allah, there is no deity but You alone, You have no partner, and that Muhammad is Your servant and Your Messenger.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/317, an-Nasa'i in Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, al-Bukhari in al-Adab al-Mufrad.

entries.titleEn: Allahumma ma amsa bi min ni'mah
entries.titleAr: اللهم ما أمسى بي من نعمة
entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَا أَمْسَى بِي مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma mā amsā bī min niʿmatin aw bi-aḥadin min khalqika fa-minka waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, falaka-l-ḥamdu wa laka-sh-shukr.
entries.translation: O Allah, whatever blessing has come to me or to any of Your creation this evening is from You alone, with no partner; so all praise is to You and all thanks.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/318, an-Nasa'i, Ibn Hibban no. 2361.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَدَنِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي سَمْعِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَصَرِي، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ، وَالْفَقْرِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī badanī, Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī samʿī, Allāhumma ʿāfinī fī baṣarī, lā ilāha illā ant. Allāhumma innī aʿūdhu bika min al-kufri wal-faqr, wa aʿūdhu bika min ʿadhāb-il-qabr, lā ilāha illā ant.
entries.translation: O Allah, grant my body health. O Allah, grant my hearing health. O Allah, grant my sight health. There is no deity but You. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave. There is no deity but You.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/324, Ahmad 5/42, an-Nasa'i no. 22, Ibn As-Sunni no. 69, al-Bukhari in al-Adab al-Mufrad.

entries.titleAr: حسبي الله لا إله إلا هو
entries.arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
entries.transliteration: Ḥasbiya-llāhu lā ilāha illā huwa, ʿalayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbu-l-ʿarshi-l-ʿaẓīm.
entries.translation: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no deity but Him. Upon Him I rely; He is Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/321, Ibn As-Sunni no. 71 (marfu').

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي دِينِي وَدُنْيَايَ وَأَهْلِي وَمَالِي، اللَّهُمَّ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَاتِي وَآمِنْ رَوْعَاتِي، اللَّهُمَّ احْفَظْنِي مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيَّ وَمِنْ خَلْفِي وَعَنْ يَمِينِي وَعَنْ شِمَالِي وَمِنْ فَوْقِي، وَأَعُوذُ بِعَظَمَتِكَ أَنْ أُغْتَالَ مِنْ تَحْتِي
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī asʾaluka-l-ʿafwa wal-ʿāfiyata fid-dunyā wal-ākhirah...
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the hereafter. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion, my worldly life, my family, and my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, protect me from in front and behind, from my right and my left, and from above. I seek refuge in Your Majesty from being killed from beneath me.
entries.source: Sahih Ibn Majah 2/332, Abu Dawud (Ibn Majah's wording).

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءاً أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ʿālima-l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fāṭira-s-samāwāti wal-arḍ, rabba kulli shayʾin wa malīkah...
entries.translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord and Master of all things, I bear witness that there is no deity but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul and from the evil of Satan and his polytheism, and from committing wrong against myself or bringing it upon any Muslim.
entries.source: Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/142, Abu Dawud.

entries.titleEn: Bismillah alladhi la yadurru ma' ismihi shay'
entries.titleAr: بسم الله الذي لا يضر مع اسمه شيء
entries.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Bismi-llāhi-lladhī lā yaḍurru maʿa ismihi shayʾun fil-arḍi walā fis-samāʾi wa huwa-s-samīʿu-l-ʿalīm.
entries.translation: In the name of Allah, with Whose name nothing in the earth or in the heavens can cause harm, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/323, At-Tirmidhi 5/465, Ibn Majah 2/332, Ahmad.

entries.arabic: رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَبِيًّا
entries.transliteration: Raḍītu billāhi rabbā, wa bil-islāmi dīnā, wa bi-Muḥammadin nabiyyā.
entries.translation: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace be upon him) as my Prophet.
entries.source: Ahmad 4/337, an-Nasa'i, Ibn As-Sunni no. 68, At-Tirmidhi 5/465.

entries.arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ، أَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِي كُلَّهُ، وَلَا تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ
entries.transliteration: Yā Ḥayyu yā Qayyūm, bi-raḥmatika astaghīth, aṣliḥ lī shaʾnī kullah, walā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata ʿayn.
entries.translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining, by Your mercy I seek help. Rectify all my affairs and do not entrust me to my soul, not even for the blink of an eye.
entries.source: Al-Hakim 1/545, with al-Albani's verification in Sahihut-Targhib 1/273.

entries.titleEn: Amsayna wa amsa al-mulk lillah, allahumma inni as'aluka khayra hadhihi-l-laylah
entries.titleAr: أمسينا وأمسى الملك لله رب العالمين
entries.arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ، فَتْحَهَا، وَنَصْرَهَا، وَنُورَهَا، وَبَرَكَتَهَا، وَهُدَاهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِيهَا وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهَا
entries.transliteration: Amsaynā wa amsa-l-mulku lillāhi rabbi-l-ʿālamīn, Allāhumma innī asʾaluka khayra hādhihi-l-laylah: fatḥahā, wa naṣrahā, wa nūrahā, wa barakatahā, wa hudāhā, wa aʿūdhu bika min sharri mā fīhā wa sharri mā baʿdahā.
entries.translation: We have entered the evening and dominion belongs to Allah, Lord of the worlds. O Allah, I ask You for the good of this night: its victory, help, light, blessings, and guidance; and I seek refuge in You from the evil in it and the evil that comes after it.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/322. Also in Ibn al-Qayyim, Zad al-Ma'ad 2/273.

entries.arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا عَلَى فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ، وَعَلَى كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلَاصِ، وَعَلَى دِينِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، وَعَلَى مِلَّةِ أَبِينَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَنِيفًا مُسْلِمًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
entries.transliteration: Amsaynā ʿalā fiṭrati-l-islām, wa ʿalā kalimati-l-ikhlāṣ, wa ʿalā dīni nabiyyinā Muḥammadin ṣalla-llāhu ʿalayhi wa sallam, wa ʿalā millati abīnā Ibrāhīm, ḥanīfan musliman, wa mā kāna mina-l-mushrikīn.
entries.translation: We have entered the evening upon the natural disposition of Islam, upon the word of pure devotion, upon the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (PBUH), and upon the way of our father Ibrahim, who was upright and Muslim, and was not of the polytheists.
entries.source: Ahmad 3/406-7, an-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi 4/209.

entries.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
entries.transliteration: Subḥāna-llāhi wa biḥamdih.
entries.translation: Glory be to Allah and praise is to Him.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 4/2071, Sahih Muslim 4/2071.

entries.titleEn: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah
entries.titleAr: لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له
entries.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
entries.transliteration: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr.
entries.translation: There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs all dominion and all praise, and He has power over all things.
entries.countDescription: 10× minimum evening, 100× preferred. Reciting 100× = reward of freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds recorded, 100 sins erased, protection from Satan that day.
entries.source: An-Nasa'i no. 24, Ahmad, Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah; al-Bukhari 4/95 and Muslim 4/2071 for the 100× version.

entries.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
entries.transliteration: Aʿūdhu bi-kalimāti-llāhi-t-tāmmāti min sharri mā khalaq.
entries.translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.
entries.countDescription: 3× evening (evening-only because the hadith specifies it; protects against stings/harm until morning)
entries.source: Ahmad 2/290, an-Nasa'i no. 590, At-Tirmidhi 3/187, Ibn As-Sunni no. 68, Ibn Majah 2/266.

entries.titleEn: Allahumma salli wa sallim 'ala nabiyyina Muhammad
entries.titleAr: اللهم صل وسلم على نبينا محمد
entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ṣalli wa sallim ʿalā nabiyyinā Muḥammad.
entries.translation: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.
entries.source: At-Tabarani (reliable chain, per al-Haythami in Majma' az-Zawa'id and al-Albani in Sahih at-Targhib). Whoever does this gains the Prophet's intercession.

afterPrayer.subtitle: Recited after every obligatory prayer (fard), in the order shown.

entries.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ ... أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ ... أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
entries.transliteration: Astaghfiru-llāh, astaghfiru-llāh, astaghfiru-llāh.
entries.translation: I seek Allah's forgiveness. I seek Allah's forgiveness. I seek Allah's forgiveness.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 1/414

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ السَّلَامُ وَمِنْكَ السَّلَامُ، تَبَارَكْتَ يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma anta-s-salām wa minka-s-salām, tabārakta yā dha-l-jalāli wal-ikrām.
entries.translation: O Allah, You are Peace and from You comes peace. Blessed are You, O Possessor of Majesty and Honor.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 1/414

entries.titleEn: La ilaha illallah... Allahumma la mani'a lima a'tayt
entries.titleAr: لا إله إلا الله وحده... اللهم لا مانع لما أعطيت
entries.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، اللَّهُمَّ لَا مَانِعَ لِمَا أَعْطَيْتَ، وَلَا مُعْطِيَ لِمَا مَنَعْتَ، وَلَا يَنْفَعُ ذَا الْجَدِّ مِنْكَ الْجَدُّ
entries.transliteration: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr. Allāhumma lā māniʿa li-mā aʿṭayt, wa lā muʿṭiya li-mā manaʿt, wa lā yanfaʿu dha-l-jaddi minka-l-jadd.
entries.translation: There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs all dominion and praise; and He has power over all things. O Allah, none can withhold what You grant, and none can grant what You withhold; and the wealth/might of the mighty cannot avail him against You.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 1/255, Sahih Muslim 1/414

entries.titleEn: La ilaha illallah... la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
entries.titleAr: لا إله إلا الله... لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
entries.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، وَلَا نَعْبُدُ إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ، لَهُ النِّعْمَةُ وَلَهُ الْفَضْلُ وَلَهُ الثَّنَاءُ الْحَسَنُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكَافِرُونَ
entries.transliteration: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr, lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā bi-llāh...
entries.translation: There is no deity but Allah alone... There is no power and no might except by Allah. There is no deity but Allah, and we worship none but Him. To Him belongs all favor, all grace, and all good praise. There is no deity but Allah; we are sincere in faith to Him, even though the disbelievers hate it.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 1/415

entries.arabic:
سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ

الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ

اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ

لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
entries.transliteration: Subḥāna-llāh (33×), al-ḥamdu lillāh (33×), Allāhu akbar (33×), then once: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr.
entries.translation: Glory be to Allah (33×). Praise be to Allah (33×). Allah is the Greatest (33×). [Once:] There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs all dominion and praise; and He has power over all things.
entries.countDescription: 33/33/33 + 1, totaling 100. Whoever says this after every prayer will be forgiven his sins even if they be like the foam of the sea.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 1/418

entries.arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۚ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ ۚ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۚ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ۚ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ۚ إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ۚ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
entries.transliteration: Qul huwa-llāhu aḥad... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-l-falaq... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-n-nās...
entries.translation: Say: He is Allah, the One... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind...
entries.countDescription: 1× after each obligatory prayer. 3× each after Fajr and Maghrib specifically.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 2/86, An-Nasa'i 3/68; al-Albani in Sahih at-Tirmidhi 2/8.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa-l-ḥayyu-l-qayyūm, lā taʾkhudhuhu sinatun walā nawm...
entries.translation: Allah! There is no deity but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him...
entries.source: An-Nasa'i, Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah no. 100; Ibn As-Sunni no. 121; al-Albani Sahih. Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after each prayer, nothing prevents him from entering Paradise except death.

entries.titleEn: La ilaha illallah... yuhyi wa yumit
entries.titleAr: لا إله إلا الله... يحيي ويميت
entries.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
entries.transliteration: Lā ilāha illa-llāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu-l-mulku wa lahu-l-ḥamd, yuḥyī wa yumīt, wa huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr.
entries.translation: There is no deity but Allah alone, with no partner; to Him belongs all dominion and praise; He gives life and causes death; and He has power over all things.
entries.source: At-Tirmidhi 5/515, Ahmad 4/227; Ibn al-Qayyim in Zad al-Ma'ad 1/300.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا، وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innī asʾaluka ʿilman nāfiʿan, wa rizqan ṭayyiban, wa ʿamalan mutaqabbalan.
entries.translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, wholesome provision, and accepted deeds.
entries.countDescription: 1× (Fajr only — recited specifically after Fajr salam)
entries.source: Sahih Ibn Majah 1/152

beforeSleep.subtitle: Recited just before sleeping, in roughly this order.

entries.arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۚ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ ۚ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۚ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ۚ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ ۚ قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ۚ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ۚ إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ۚ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ۚ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
entries.transliteration: Qul huwa-llāhu aḥad... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-l-falaq... / Qul aʿūdhu bi-rabbi-n-nās...
entries.translation: Say: He is Allah, the One... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak... / Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind... Method: Bring the palms together, blow lightly into them, recite each surah, then wipe over the body — head and face first, then the front of the body. Repeat the full sequence 3 times.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim 4/1723

entries.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
entries.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa-l-ḥayyu-l-qayyūm...
entries.translation: Allah! There is no deity but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining...
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari. A guardian from Allah will remain with you and Satan will not approach you until you wake.

entries.arabic:
آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ ۚ وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ

لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ۗ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ۖ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ۚ أَنتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
entries.transliteration: Āmana-r-rasūlu bimā unzila ilayhi min rabbihi wal-muʾminūn... / Lā yukallifu-llāhu nafsan illā wusʿahā...
entries.translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and the believers... / Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity...
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari (Fath al-Bari 9/94), Sahih Muslim 1/554. These two verses will suffice him.

entries.arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّي وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِي، وَبِكَ أَرْفَعُهُ، فَإِنْ أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فَارْحَمْهَا، وَإِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
entries.transliteration: Bi-smika rabbī waḍaʿtu janbī wa bika arfaʿuh, fa-in amsakta nafsī farḥamhā, wa in arsaltahā faḥfaẓhā bimā taḥfaẓu bihi ʿibādaka-ṣ-ṣāliḥīn.
entries.translation: In Your name, my Lord, I lay myself down, and in Your name I rise. If You take my soul, have mercy on it; and if You release it, protect it as You protect Your righteous servants.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 1/126, Sahih Muslim 4/2084

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِي وَأَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْيَاهَا، إِنْ أَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَإِنْ أَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma innaka khalaqta nafsī wa anta tawaffāhā, laka mamātuhā wa maḥyāhā, in aḥyaytahā faḥfaẓhā, wa in amattahā faghfir lahā. Allāhumma innī asʾaluka-l-ʿāfiyah.
entries.translation: O Allah, You created my soul and You take it back. To You is its life and its death. If You give it life, protect it; if You cause it to die, forgive it. O Allah, I ask You for well-being.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2083, Ahmad 2/79

entries.titleAr: اللهم قني عذابك يوم تبعث عبادك
entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ قِنِي عَذَابَكَ يَوْمَ تَبْعَثُ عِبَادَكَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma qinī ʿadhābaka yawma tabʿathu ʿibādak.
entries.translation: O Allah, protect me from Your punishment on the Day You resurrect Your servants.
entries.countDescription: 3× (lying down, placing right hand under cheek)
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/311; al-Albani Sahih in Sahih at-Tirmidhi 3/143.

entries.arabic: بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا
entries.transliteration: Bi-smika-llāhumma amūtu wa aḥyā.
entries.translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari (Hisn 105)

entries.titleAr: سبحان الله ٣٣ / الحمد لله ٣٣ / الله أكبر ٣٤
entries.arabic:
سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ

الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ

اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
entries.transliteration: Subḥāna-llāh (33×), al-ḥamdu lillāh (33×), Allāhu akbar (34×).
entries.translation: Glory be to Allah (33). Praise be to Allah (33). Allah is the Greatest (34).
entries.countDescription: 33/33/34, totaling 100. The Prophet taught Fatimah and Ali this is better than a servant they had asked for.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari (Fath al-Bari 7/71), Sahih Muslim 4/2091

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَاوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ، فَالِقَ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَى، وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ وَالْفُرْقَانِ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَنْتَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الْأَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْآخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَيْءٌ، وَأَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُونَكَ شَيْءٌ، اقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَأَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma rabba-s-samāwāti-s-sabʿ wa rabba-l-ʿarshi-l-ʿaẓīm, rabbanā wa rabba kulli shayʾ...
entries.translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Magnificent Throne, our Lord and Lord of all things... pay off our debts and free us from poverty.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2084

entries.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا، وَكَفَانَا، وَآوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لَا كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلَا مُؤْوِيَ
entries.transliteration: Al-ḥamdu lillāhi-lladhī aṭʿamanā wa saqānā, wa kafānā, wa āwānā, fakam mimman lā kāfiya lahu wa lā muʾwī.
entries.translation: Praise is to Allah Who has fed us and given us drink, sufficed us and given us shelter. How many are without a sufficer or a shelter.
entries.source: Sahih Muslim 4/2085

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءاً أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma ʿālima-l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati fāṭira-s-samāwāti wal-arḍ, rabba kulli shayʾin wa malīkah...
entries.translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord and Master of all things, I bear witness that there is no deity but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul and from the evil of Satan and his polytheism, and from committing wrong against myself or bringing it upon any Muslim.
entries.source: Abu Dawud 4/317, At-Tirmidhi 3/142

entries.titleEn: Recite Surah as-Sajdah (32) and Surah al-Mulk (67)
entries.arabic: سُورَةُ السَّجْدَةِ (٣٢) وَسُورَةُ الْمُلْكِ (٦٧)
entries.transliteration: Sūrat as-Sajdah (32) wa Sūrat al-Mulk (67).
entries.translation: Recite Surah as-Sajdah (chapter 32) and Surah al-Mulk (chapter 67) before sleeping.
entries.source: At-Tirmidhi, an-Nasa'i; al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami' as-Saghir 4/255.

entries.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَسْلَمْتُ نَفْسِي إِلَيْكَ، وَفَوَّضْتُ أَمْرِي إِلَيْكَ، وَوَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِي إِلَيْكَ، وَأَلْجَأْتُ ظَهْرِي إِلَيْكَ، رَغْبَةً وَرَهْبَةً إِلَيْكَ، لَا مَلْجَأَ وَلَا مَنْجَا مِنْكَ إِلَّا إِلَيْكَ، آمَنْتُ بِكِتَابِكَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَ، وَبِنَبِيِّكَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلْتَ
entries.transliteration: Allāhumma aslamtu nafsī ilayk, wa fawwaḍtu amrī ilayk, wa wajjahtu wajhī ilayk, wa aljaʾtu ẓahrī ilayk, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayk, lā maljaʾa wa lā manjā minka illā ilayk, āmantu bi-kitābika-lladhī anzalt, wa bi-nabiyyika-lladhī arsalt.
entries.translation: O Allah, I submit my soul to You, entrust my affair to You, turn my face to You, and lay my back upon You — out of hope in You and fear of You. There is no refuge and no escape from You except to You. I believe in Your Book that You revealed and in Your Prophet whom You sent.
entries.countDescription: 1× (just before sleeping). Whoever recites this and dies that night dies upon fitrah.
entries.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim
